libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image, environment variable, or system property was found to be required. Generated tests should use the 2.0.0 API: obtain an injected SiteRenderer through a Plexus/Sisu test container instead of new DefaultSiteRenderer(); create temporary site root/src-site directories and SiteRenderingContext.SiteDirectory entries; use DocumentRenderingContext where older tests used RenderingContext. Older 1.x APIs such as SkinResourceLoader, enableLogging, and setUsingDefaultTemplate are gone.

deterministic_setup.reason: The site renderer API exposes Maven Artifact for skin-based context creation, but the artifact POM marks maven-artifact as provided, so it is not present in the default Gradle test classpath.

DefaultSiteRenderer is a Plexus/Sisu component with injected Doxia, parser manager, velocity, and container fields; upstream tests use Plexus testing support to obtain an injected SiteRenderer instead of constructing it directly.
The 2.0.0 POM states Doxia modules are no longer in core and must be declared so component descriptors are picked up; APT rendering is a common site-renderer path and is test-scoped upstream.
XDoc is one of the upstream test-scoped parser modules needed for realistic site document discovery/rendering beyond the single transitive XHTML5 parser.
FML is one of the upstream test-scoped parser modules needed for realistic Doxia site-renderer parser-module coverage.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8134 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven.doxia:doxia-site-renderer:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The existing compile failure is primarily stale 1.9.1 test code against the 2.0.0 API; deterministic setup will not by itself fix those source-level API changes.
The extra parser modules are only needed if tests exercise real document discovery/rendering for APT, XDoc, or FML; very shallow tests might compile without them but would provide weaker coverage.
Native Image and local Gradle verification remain authoritative because Plexus/Sisu and Velocity component discovery can expose additional reachability requirements.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Doxia Site Renderer 2.0.0 moved parser modules out of core and is normally exercised through Maven/Plexus component injection; meaningful rendering coverage likely needs test-scoped parser/component dependencies beyond the default scaffold.
